author: @ShubhamChauhan-b3m
comment:
Short Note on Artificial Intelligence (AI)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a branch of computer science that enables machines and computers to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence. These tasks include learning, reasoning, problem-solving, decision-making, understanding language, and recognizing images or speech.

AI is used in many areas of daily life, such as virtual assistants, online recommendations, self-driving vehicles, healthcare, education, and robotics. It helps improve efficiency, accuracy, and productivity.

There are different types of AI, ranging from simple systems designed for specific tasks to advanced systems that can learn from data and improve over time. As technology continues to develop, AI is becoming an important part of modern society and has the potential to transform many industries.

Conclusion: AI is a powerful technology that helps machines think and act intelligently, making life easier and solving complex problems.

author: @dianamcginnis8694
comment: In this world of artificial intelligence, WHAT IF there were a super-computer with such a vast understanding of the text, original intent, history, previous interpretations (and misinterpretations) etc. of the United States Constitution that it would be recognized as the preeminent authority on that document?  Although the super-computer's renderings would be non-binding, they would be widely available  to government officeholders and to the general public and could be used to gauge how closely Congress and the Courts and the Executive coincided with or diverged from its renderings.  WHAT IF?  This interesting question is the basis for my short story "THE SENTRY; The Story of the Constitution-Reading Super-Computer".in my book "POTPOURRI; A Collection of Short Stories by Diana McGinnis."

author: @AmareLemma-s5e
comment:
That's a good explanation. All the concerns mentioned like influencing people to a certain political ideology, substitution of the workforce with machines, fraud, ... and the likes are real. But the most important limitation (even deficiency) of Machines and Artificial Intelligence (AI) will be its inability to process information across disciplines (and/or to engage a multi-disciplinary team of experties). Moreover, the ability of AI to evolve with and/or at a pace and/or in synchrony with the evolution of living beings, Humans included, remains doubtful for the simple reason that such biological transformations are influenced by a multitude of "reversible" and vicious factors (that influence bothways ...  humans changing their environment and humans and other living things adapting to the new environment and readapting ...). But there is one critical issue many of us OVERLOOK when it comes to the use of Artifical Intelligence and associated Technologies: DISUSE ATROPHY!!!!

DISUSE ATROPHY simply means loss of the anatomo-physiological features of our bodily functions and parts that have been MAINTAINED in a state of PROTRACTED INACTIVITY. 

[For every activity replaced by Machines and AI, the corresponding anatomo-physiological features will be compromised that eventually lead to a permanent and irreversible functional and structural lose]. ...

NATURE IS VERY CONSERVATIVE; SO ARE THE LAWS OF NATURE.

EXISTENCE Is a HUGE Investment On The Part Of NATURE To Easily LET GO and thence, LOSS is MAINTAINED to the ABSOLUTE MINIMUM in Any of the NATURAL EVENTS and/or PHENOMENA ... as can be Gleaned (or Surmised) from observations of the MULTIPLICITY Of REDUNDENCIES and LANDMARKS Of The Universe -  Planets, Gene/ DNA, ...!!!

Stop walking and we lose the ability to walk and eventually, ... the very legs we used to walk with (Nature Conserves the LEGS).
Stop writing with our fingers and we lose the ability to write and eventually, ... the very fingers we used to hold the pens (Nature Conserves the FINGERS).
Stop memorizing the phone numbers of our friends and family members and we lose the ability to remember things and eventually, ... the very brain part/s involved with this particular function (Nature Conserves the BRAIN parts).
Stop doing Math by ourselves and we lose the analytical part of our brain that eventually regress/ degenerate (Nature Conserves the CEREBRUM).
Stop ... and we lose ... NATURE CONSERVES ...
Stop ... and we lose ... NATURE CONSERVES ...
Stop ... and we lose ... NATURE CONSERVES ...

These are NOT Imaginery or Paranoic THREATES.
They are NOT Far-fetched and Probabilistic PREDICTIONS.

ONE GENERATION is More Than Enough to EXPERIENCE these CALAMITOUS Transformations.

We have to Always Consider DISUSE ATROPHY in any of the DECISONS we make regarding the AMOUNT and/or LEVEL of TECHNOLOGY to INTRODUCE in Our DAY-TO-DAY LIFE.
